name: jail mail boundary puncture

phase_0.boundary_condition: The reinforced mailroom security checkpoint and institutional intake ledger

VINIT.justification: Raw mail exists in an uninspected state before entering the facility perimeter.

TANCH.justification: The physical and procedural checkpoint that contains the entire inspection system.

AFWD.chosen_element: Forwarding approved letters to cell blocks
AFWD.justification: The directed transformation of cleared mail toward its final inmate recipient.

AREV.chosen_element: Reversing confiscated contraband to sender or destruction
AREV.justification: The descent and rejection of prohibited items back out of the facility.

CLINK.chosen_element: Sequential logging and sorting of the intake queue
CLINK.justification: The chained procedural steps of receiving and preparing mail for inspection.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: The inmate verified identity matching the recipient list
IMSCRIB.justification: The self-referential confirmation that the mail belongs to a recognized entity within the system.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: The security inspection gate separating cleared and suspicious streams
FSPLIT.justification: The branching point where a single mail batch divides into approved and prohibited paths.

FFUSE.chosen_element: The daily mailroom ledger reconciliation of total processed mail
FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of the original batch by summing cleared and confiscated items for the official record.

EVALT.chosen_element: Stamping correspondence as cleared for delivery
EVALT.justification: The affirmative evaluation confirming the item meets all institutional policies.

EVALF.chosen_element: Flagging items as contraband or policy violations
EVALF.justification: The negative evaluation identifying prohibited materials or unauthorized senders.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Mail legally opened by guards yet delivered as private correspondence
ENGAGR.justification: A paradice where the mail is simultaneously violated by state inspection and protected by inmate privacy rights.

IFIX.chosen_element: The permanent institutional log of deliveries and confiscations
IFIX.justification: The append-only record that permanently fixes the outcome of every inspection event.

phase_3.void_description: Uninspected and unlogged mail sitting outside the security perimeter
phase_3.true_description: Cleared correspondence approved and routed for inmate delivery
phase_3.false_description: Confiscated contraband rejected and logged for return or destruction
phase_3.both_description: Mail simultaneously violated by guard inspection and protected by inmate privacy rights

steps.domain_action: Raw unsorted mail arrives at the facility gate.
The perimeter mailroom checkpoint establishes the security boundary.
Mail is logged into the intake queue for processing.
The inspection gate separates the batch into cleared and suspicious streams.
Cleared correspondence is stamped as approved for delivery.
Approved letters are forwarded to the inmate cell blocks.
Suspicious items are flagged as contraband.
Confiscated items are reversed back to the sender or destroyed.
The mailroom ledger reconciles cleared and confiscated items into the total daily batch.
Legal mail opened by guards is delivered as private correspondence.
The permanent confiscation and delivery log is written to the institutional record.
The inmate verified identity is confirmed against the approved recipient list.
The mailroom cycle closes and seals the perimeter for the next intake.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The institutional mailroom policy manual and censorship guidelines
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Physical transport carts and intercom buzzers between checkpoints
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The centralized intake ledger and physical evidence lockers
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The daily intake bell schedule and guard shift rotations
phase_5.alfs_store: The Department of Corrections master inmate registry and approved contact list

phase_6.cycle_cost: Guard labor hours and administrative logging overhead
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Unsorted mail batch outside the security boundary
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Processed mail batch with permanent ledger entries and separated contraband
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The physical mass of mail is conserved while its state and location change within the closed institutional system.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IFIX → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: jail mail boundary puncture

-- Expected tier: O₂

     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑱  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑡 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition

--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def jail_mail_boundary_puncture_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Raw unsorted mail arrives at the facility gate.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The perimeter mailroom checkpoint establishes the security boundary.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Mail is logged into the intake queue for processing.)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (The inspection gate separates the batch into cleared and suspicious streams.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Cleared correspondence is stamped as approved for delivery.)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Approved letters are forwarded to the inmate cell blocks.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Suspicious items are flagged as contraband.)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Confiscated items are reversed back to the sender or destroyed.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The mailroom ledger reconciles cleared and confiscated items into the total daily batch.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Legal mail opened by guards is delivered as private correspondence.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑠)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The permanent confiscation and delivery log is written to the institutional r...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The inmate verified identity is confirmed against the approved recipient list.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [12] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The mailroom cycle closes and seals the perimeter for the next intake.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def jail_mail_boundary_puncture_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (jail_mail_boundary_puncture_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def jail_mail_boundary_puncture_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (jail_mail_boundary_puncture_protocol).restrictToEVALF
